content.problemStatement:
Say, $S_A$ is the set of integers divisible by $A$, similarly define $S_B$ and $S_C$. \
Say, $S_{A, B}$ is the set of integers divisible both $A$ and $B$ simultaneously, similarly define $S_{B, C}$ and $S_{A,C}$. \
Say, $S_{A, B, C}$ is the set of integers divisible by all $A$, $B$ and $C$ simultaneously.

The number of integers divisible by at least one of $A$, $B$ or $C$ can be given by the help of inclusion-exclusion principle on three sets.

$\rightarrow$ $|S_A|$ $+$ $|S_B|$ $+$ $|S_C|$ $-$ $|S_{A, B}|$ $-$ $|S_{B, C}|$ $-$ $|S_{A, C}|$ $+$ $|S_{A, B, C}|$
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 1000$
- $2 \leq N \leq 10^5$
- $1 \leq A_i \leq 10^9$
- The sum of $N$ across all test cases does not exceed $10^5$
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases. The description of the test cases follows.
- The first line of each test case contains a single integer $N$, denoting the length of array $A$.
- The second line of each test case contains $N$ space-separated integers $A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_N$ â€” denoting the array $A$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the maximum value of $A_1+A_N$ you can get after several right rotations.
